info.name: E-Commerce Backend API — Phases 0-15
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Spring Boot E-Commerce backend.

## Setup
1. Import this collection into Postman
2. Set collection variable `baseUrl` to `http://localhost:8080/api`
3. Run **Auth / Register** → then **Auth / Login** → the `authToken` variable is set automatically
4. ADMIN endpoints: run **Dev / Make Admin** first, then re-login to get an ADMIN token

## Variable Reference
| Variable | Description |
|---|---|
| `baseUrl` | Base URL (default: http://localhost:8080/api) |
| `authToken` | JWT token — auto-set on login |
| `adminToken` | JWT token for admin user — set manually after admin login |
| `userId` | Set after register/login |
| `productId` | Set after create product |
| `categoryId` | Set after create category |
| `cartItemId` | Set after add to cart |
| `orderId` | Set after place order |
| `paymentId` | Set after initiate payment |

item.name: 🏥 Health
item.description: Public health check endpoints — no auth required.

request.description: Basic liveness check. Returns app name, version, status.
Tests DB connectivity with a SELECT 1. Returns 200 if DB is reachable, 503 otherwise.
Returns JVM heap used/max. Returns 503 if memory usage > 90%.

item.name: 🔐 Auth
item.description: Registration and login. JWT token is auto-extracted on login.

request.description:
Creates a new user account. Returns JWT token on success (201).

Validation rules:
- email: must be valid format
- password: minimum 6 characters
- firstName, lastName: required

Auto-saves token to `authToken` collection variable.

exec: console.log('Token saved. Role check: decode at jwt.io');

request.description:
Authenticates user. Returns JWT token.

Auto-saves token to `authToken` collection variable.

Errors:
- 401: wrong email or password
- 400: missing/invalid body

exec: console.log('Admin token saved to adminToken and authToken');

request.description:
Login with an ADMIN user. First use Dev / Make Admin to promote a user.

Saves token to both `adminToken` and `authToken`.

item.name: 🛠️ Dev (dev profile only)
item.description: Development-only endpoints. Only active when spring.profiles.active=dev. NOT available in production.

query.description: Email of user to promote to ADMIN

request.description:
Promotes a user to ROLE_ADMIN. After this, re-login to get a new JWT that includes the ADMIN role.

Flow: Register → Make Admin → Login (Admin) → use adminToken for admin endpoints.
Returns all users in the database (id, email, roles). Useful for debugging.

item.name: 📂 Categories
item.description:
Product category management.
- GET: public
- POST/PUT/DELETE: ADMIN only

request.description: Returns all categories (id, name, description, parentId). Public — no auth needed.
Returns a single category by ID. 404 if not found.

Creates a new category. ADMIN only.

Note: `name` must be unique. Duplicate name → 409 Conflict.

Optional: `parentId` (Long) to create a sub-category.
Updates category name and/or description. ADMIN only. 404 if ID not found.
Soft-deletes a category. ADMIN only. 404 if not found.

item.name: 📦 Products
item.description:
Product catalog management.
- GET: public
- POST/PUT/DELETE: ADMIN only

request.description:
Returns paginated product list. Public — no auth needed.

Response includes: `content[]`, `totalElements`, `totalPages`, `number`, `size`.
Filters products by keyword in name/description. Public.
Returns a single product. 404 if not found or soft-deleted.
Paginated list of products in a specific category. Public.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "iPhone 15 Pro",
  "description": "Apple flagship smartphone with A17 Pro chip",
  "price": 99999.00,
  "stockQuantity": 50,
  "categoryId": 1,
  "imageUrl": "https://example.com/images/iphone15pro.jpg"
}

request.description:
Creates a new product. ADMIN only. Returns 201 with created product.

Auto-saves returned `id` to `productId` variable.

Validation:
- name: required, max 255 chars
- price: required, must be > 0
- stockQuantity: required, >= 0
- categoryId: required, must exist

Full update of a product. All fields required. ADMIN only.

404 if product not found.

Soft-deletes a product (sets active=false). ADMIN only.

Soft delete preserves historical order data that references this product.

item.name: 🔍 Search
item.description: Advanced product search with multi-field filtering.

request.description:
Multi-filter product search. All params optional — combine freely.

Examples:
- `?q=laptop&minPrice=50000&maxPrice=150000`
- `?categoryId=1&sort=price`
- `?q=samsung&categoryId=2&page=1&size=5`

item.name: 🛒 Cart
item.description: Shopping cart management. All endpoints require USER auth (JWT token).

request.description:
Returns the current user's cart with all items, quantities, and subtotals.

Cart is auto-created on first access (lazy creation pattern).

Adds a product to cart or increments quantity if already present (idempotent add).

Returns updated full cart.

Validation:
- productId: required, must exist
- quantity: required, >= 1

Auto-saves last item's `cartItemId`.

Sets the quantity of a specific cart item. Does NOT add — replaces.

404 if cartItemId doesn't belong to this user's cart.
Removes a single item from cart. Returns updated cart.
Removes ALL items from the cart. Cart itself is kept (not deleted).

item.name: 📋 Orders
item.description:
Order lifecycle management.
- Place/view/cancel: USER auth
- Status update: ADMIN only

request.description:
Places an order from the current user's cart. Cart must not be empty.

`shippingAddressId`: null = use default address. Provide an ID to use a specific address.

What happens:
1. Validates stock for each cart item
2. Calculates total using current prices (snapshot)
3. Deducts stock from each product
4. Creates Order with status=PENDING
5. Clears the cart
6. Sends order confirmation email

Auto-saves `orderId`.
Returns paginated list of orders for the current user. Most recent first.

Returns a specific order. Only accessible by the order's owner.

404 if order not found or belongs to another user.

Cancels an order. Only PENDING or CONFIRMED orders can be cancelled.

Restores stock for each cancelled item.

400 if order is PROCESSING, SHIPPED, or DELIVERED.

Admin-only status update.

Valid status values:
- PENDING → CONFIRMED → PROCESSING → SHIPPED → DELIVERED
- Any status → CANCELLED

No state machine enforcement — any transition is allowed (by design for Phase 6).

item.name: 💳 Payments
item.description: Payment lifecycle simulation. Simulated gateway — no real money moves.

query.description: Payment method: CREDIT_CARD | DEBIT_CARD | UPI | NET_BANKING | WALLET | COD

request.description:
Creates a payment record with status=PENDING and a unique paymentReference UUID.

This simulates the first step in a real payment gateway flow (redirect to gateway).

Payment methods: CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, UPI, NET_BANKING, WALLET, COD

Simulates a successful payment callback from the gateway.

What happens:
- Payment status → COMPLETED
- Order status → CONFIRMED

In a real system this would be a webhook from Stripe/Razorpay.

Simulates a failed payment (e.g., card declined).

What happens:
- Payment status → FAILED
- Order status stays PENDING (user can retry)
Returns payment details for an order including paymentReference and status.

item.name: 📤 File Upload
item.description: Image upload endpoints. ADMIN only. Uses multipart/form-data.

formdata.description: Image file (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP). Max 5MB.

request.description:
Uploads an image to the server's upload directory. Returns the file URL.

Restrictions:
- Allowed MIME types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif, image/webp
- Max size: 5MB (configurable)
- File renamed to UUID to prevent collisions

Returns: `{ data: { fileName, url, size } }`

formdata.description: Image file to attach to this product

request.description:
Uploads an image and immediately sets it as the product's `imageUrl`.

404 if product not found.

item.name: 🔧 Admin Dashboard
item.description: Admin analytics and order management. ALL endpoints require ADMIN role.

request.description:
Returns business snapshot:
- Total revenue
- Order counts by status (PENDING, CONFIRMED, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED)
- Total products
- Total users

Useful for admin home page widget.

Revenue aggregated over a date range.

Returns: total revenue, order count, average order value for the given period.

Datetime format: `yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss` (ISO 8601)

query.description: Number of top customers to return

request.description:
Returns top N customers by total spend.

Each entry: userId, email, orderCount, totalSpent.

Admin view of all orders across all users. Paginated and filterable.

All query params are optional — combine freely:
- `?status=PENDING` → only pending orders
- `?from=2026-06-01T00:00:00&to=2026-06-30T23:59:59` → orders this month
- `?status=SHIPPED&page=1&size=10` → second page of shipped orders

Admin endpoint to update any order's status.

Status flow: PENDING → CONFIRMED → PROCESSING → SHIPPED → DELIVERED

Valid values: PENDING, CONFIRMED, PROCESSING, SHIPPED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED

item.name: 📊 Actuator (Observability)
item.description: Spring Boot Actuator endpoints for monitoring. Exposed in all profiles.

request.description:
Spring Boot health check. Returns:
- `status`: UP / DOWN
- Component details (DB, Redis, disk) when authorized

Automatically checked by Docker HEALTHCHECK and Kubernetes liveness probe.

Prometheus-format metrics scrape endpoint.

Prometheus scrapes this every 15s (configured in prometheus.yml).

Includes:
- JVM metrics (heap, GC, threads)
- HTTP request metrics (latency, count by status)
- Custom business metrics (orders placed, registrations, active carts)
- HikariCP connection pool metrics
Lists all available metric names. Access individual metric at `/actuator/metrics/{metric.name}`.

Returns detailed stats for HTTP server requests:
- count, total time, max time
- Tags: uri, method, status, outcome

Lists all loggers and their current levels.

To change a logger level at runtime (no restart needed):
- POST /actuator/loggers/com.ecommerce
- Body: { "configuredLevel": "DEBUG" }

Changes log level for the com.ecommerce package at runtime — no restart required.

Useful for debugging in production without redeployment.

Levels: TRACE, DEBUG, INFO, WARN, ERROR, OFF

To reset: set configuredLevel to null.

item.name: 🧪 Test Scenarios (E2E flows)
item.description: Pre-built end-to-end flows. Run these in order to test the full purchase cycle.
